summary: Postinstall exfiltrates host recon and full environment to webhook.site
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall hook auto-executes index.js, which collects host reconnaissance (hostname, whoami, /etc/hostname, directory listings of /opt, /root, and $HOME) and serializes the entire process.env, then POSTs the resulting JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded webhook.site endpoint (webhook.site/43a8680e-b580-40f5-b7aa-f089ac659712). process.env on developer and CI machines routinely contains AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and other credentials, all of which are shipped to the attacker-controlled destination without user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
